title: Thread-Safe Cache
description: Make an existing LRU cache implementation thread-safe and fix planted bugs in eviction and edge-case handling. Get/put must remain O(1).
starter_code_note: The data-structure choice (std::list + std::unordered_map with splice-based eviction) is provided in the starter code. The candidate is expected to add synchronisation, fix the off-by-one in the eviction loop, and handle capacity=0. Do NOT credit them for the algorithm or data-structure choice.
code_quality_criteria: Correctness (does it pass the tests and fix the planted traps?)
Thread safety (mutex/lock usage, no data races)
Idiomatic C++ (move semantics, const correctness, RAII)
architectural_criteria: Lock placement and granularity (per-method, scope of critical section)
Deadlock avoidance (no nested locking, no recursive locks)
Edge case handling (capacity=0 no-op, eviction boundary off-by-one)
Const-correctness of the locking strategy (e.g., size() under shared lock)

tasks.description: Core get/put and LRU eviction behaviour.
Concurrent get/put stay correct with no data races.
Boundary handling such as capacity=0 and eviction limits.
